last_excerpt: --- Laundry is one kind of residue but there are others. The patterns on a keyboard: some keys shining smooth, letters rubbing off, islands of skin oil only visible at a certain angle. The Q always looks older than the T, although it’s pressed less. That doesn’t make sense but it’s true on this one. There’s residue on phone screens, too. A map of every swipe and press, only visible in the light at dusk. Lists of residues: — black dust at the bottom of a backpack — pinkish eraser shavings caught in binder rings — yellowed tape at the edge of a poster left too long — coffee rings on the underside of a mug — salt dried to a white bloom on leather shoes — dried soap scum in the base of a bottle Some residues grow, some shrink, some just persist, outlasting the thing that made them.
last_concepts: the way a cocoon dissolves from the inside as the moth develops
how a mechanical watch escapement releases energy one tick at a time
the Braille system and the logic of six dots
held door from too far away
the way a river erodes the outside of a bend and deposits on the inside
a driveway crack filled with tar
the particular gray of dryer lint
